proposed_base_material_variables.name: specimen_thickness
proposed_base_material_variables.definition: The nominal thickness of the alloy sheet or plate material used for the test specimens.

proposed_base_material_variables.name: heat_identification
proposed_base_material_variables.definition: A unique identifier or number assigned to a specific batch or melt of the alloy, used to distinguish between different productions of the same alloy grade.

proposed_conditioned_material_variables.name: inter_step_surface_refreshment
proposed_conditioned_material_variables.definition: Indicates whether the specimen surface is resanded and cleaned between consecutive temperature steps in a cyclic immersion test.

proposed_conditioned_material_variables.name: crevice_former_type
proposed_conditioned_material_variables.definition: Description of the material and geometry used to create the crevice, such as TFE-fluorocarbon washers or multiple crevice devices.

proposed_experiment_variables.name: critical_crevice_temperature (CCT)
proposed_experiment_variables.definition: The minimum temperature at which crevice corrosion initiation is observed in the specified environment.

proposed_experiment_variables.name: initial_immersion_temperature
proposed_experiment_variables.definition: The starting temperature of the test solution when the specimen is first immersed prior to subsequent temperature increments.

proposed_experiment_variables.name: temperature_step_increment
proposed_experiment_variables.definition: The fixed temperature increase (e.g., 2.5 °C) applied to the test solution after an exposure period where no corrosion was observed.

proposed_experiment_variables.name: step_exposure_duration
proposed_experiment_variables.definition: The length of time (e.g., 24 hours) the specimen is exposed to the environment at each individual temperature step.
